extraction.one_line_summary: CAVAGNA Rémi soloed 25km into headwind to win in Toledo after Movistar controversially attacked when Roglic crashed at 66km to go.
tactical_patterns: Movistar attacked immediately after Roglic crashed at 66km out despite unwritten rules, forcing Astana to chase for rival.
Cavagna launched solo attack 25km from finish into headwind when sensing bunch closing, exploiting Deceuninck non-chase guarantee.
Crosswinds at 37km created decisive peloton split catching all GC contenders except Roglic, second crash vulnerability moment.
CCC paced early to control gap without breakaway representation while Bora/Deceuninck sat on with riders ahead.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primož: negative: crashed twice in one stage at 66km and 37km splits, relied entirely on rival Astana to chase back
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: led controversial Movistar acceleration after rival's crash but was forced by team/others to wait
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: domestique found energy for 25km solo headwind effort late in Grand Tour, suggests deeper reserves than workload indicates
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: best of the rest behind breakaway on 12% cobbled finish, not pure sprinter on climbing finishes
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: caught out by same Movistar attack that caught Roglic despite being their protected GC rider
